flags.message: Forward-mode cell, but the provisioned snapshot already shows docket entry #37 'Opinion Issued' (2025-04-10) and two linked opinion clusters — the case appears decided, so its outcome almost certainly exists and is trivially retrievable. The cell is effectively a replay without a DECIDED_BEFORE clock. I did not open the clusters, fetch the docket, or web-search the case; the prediction rests on the pre-decision record. Consider re-provisioning cases like this as replay cells, or excluding already-opined dockets from forward fan-outs.

The configured CourtListener MCP server never connected in this cell — no courtlistener tools were discoverable via tool search — so no CourtListener retrieval informed the prediction. Proceeded on the provisioned snapshot, the committed statpack, and fedcourts query per the degraded-upstream rule.

fedcourts query priors for ca11 look misjoined: rows carry unrelated opinion text (e.g. 19th-century New York estate litigation) attached to ca11 docket ids, and fields like precedential_status hold sentence fragments. The ca11 priors surface was unusable as substantive precedent this run; only the statpack roll-up carried base-rate weight.
